sourceNotes: Historical-inspired, fictionalized for gameplay; does not use real party names in playable text.
Covers 2006-2010 Tamil Nadu events with expanded categories: corruption/scams, infrastructure, welfare, health, and law & order.

Key anchors include 2006 assembly hung polls, DMK minority cabinet, free color TV and Rs 2/kg rice rollouts, Hogenakkal water row, Madurai newspaper office attack, Sethusamudram row, Sri Lankan civil war protests, Stalin's Deputy CM promotion, Semmozhi conference, Pennagaram by-election, and 2G spectrum controversy.

defaults.reactionRule: Each news item contains its own 3 active reactions plus no_comment. Reaction weights and effects vary by issue profile.
defaults.thirdPartyReactionRule: THIRD_PARTY can use opposition-compatible and neutral reactions.

newsItems.title: DMK Manifesto Promises Free TVs and Rs 2/kg Rice
newsItems.description: The DMK releases its assembly election manifesto, promising to distribute free color televisions to every household and supply rice at Rs 2 per kg under the public distribution system.

reactionOptions.text: Dismiss the promises as fiscally ruinous and warn that the state will face massive debt.

risk.badOutcome: Rural voters show high enthusiasm for the welfare promises in initial polls.

reactionOptions.text: Highlight the television as an education tool and the cheap rice as a vital step to eliminate hunger.

risk.badOutcome: Finance columnists write critical editorials warning of budget deficits.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that freebies be limited strictly to below-poverty-line (BPL) families to preserve the state budget.

risk.badOutcome: Voters find targeted criteria confusing, favoring universal distribution schemes.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: PMK and MDMK Alliance Negotiations Intensify
newsItems.description: With assembly elections declared, regional groups PMK and MDMK hold parallel alliance negotiations with both major fronts, seeking maximum seat shares.

reactionOptions.text: Offer cabinet berth guarantees and key district seats to secure the partners within the alliance.

risk.badOutcome: Internal party MLAs express anger over losing their sitting constituencies.

reactionOptions.text: Demand strict seat-sharing limits to preserve the party's own grassroots candidate strength.

risk.badOutcome: Partner parties walk away, opening talks with the rival coalition.

reactionOptions.text: Propose establishing a post-poll steering committee to distribute powers equitably after elections.

risk.badOutcome: Steering committee talks break down over coordinator choices.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: MDMK Switches Sides; Joins AIADMK Alliance
newsItems.description: In a major pre-election blow to the DMK-led DPA, Vaiko's MDMK formally switches sides and joins the AIADMK alliance after receiving a seat offer of 35 seats.

reactionOptions.text: Welcome the MDMK into the fold and launch a joint campaign highlighting the combined front's strength.

risk.badOutcome: Sitting MLAs in the allotted 35 seats protest and refuse to coordinate campaign work.

reactionOptions.text: Dismiss the departure as a minor loss, stating that MDMK's voter base has already shifted to the DPA.

risk.badOutcome: Cadres in southern districts report increased local contest challenges.

reactionOptions.text: Propose holding voter loyalty rallies in the contested districts to reassure grassroots supporters.

risk.badOutcome: Heavy rain delays key loyalty rallies, reducing voter attendance.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Campaign Highlights: Freebies vs Fiscal Prudence
newsItems.description: The assembly campaign debates center on the DMK's free TV and cheap rice promises versus the AIADMK's warnings of budget deficits and inflation.

reactionOptions.text: Focus on the state's industrial investments, IT parks, and road connectivity constructed under the regime.

risk.badOutcome: Voters find infrastructure arguments less appealing than direct household welfare promises.

reactionOptions.text: Highlight the direct economic relief that free TVs and Rs 2/kg rice will bring to rural families.

risk.badOutcome: Critics warn that distribution logistics will lead to corruption and leaks.

reactionOptions.text: Propose establishing a bipartisan economic advisory council to review welfare funding sources.

risk.badOutcome: Voters view the advisory proposal as a sign of hesitation to deliver benefits.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: DMK Minority Government Sworn In with Congress Support
newsItems.description: The assembly election results lead to a hung house. The DMK emerges as the single largest party and forms a minority government supported from outside by the Congress.

reactionOptions.text: Assert that the coalition is stable and that all welfare promises will be fulfilled starting this week.

risk.badOutcome: Congress allies demand a share in the cabinet, creating initial friction.

reactionOptions.text: Label the new regime a weak, dependent government that will collapse at the first disagreement.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party releases consensus development agenda, gaining positive press.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a formal legislative coordination committee representing all alliance partners to draft bills.

risk.badOutcome: Delays in selecting the committee coordinator stall initial policy reviews.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: CM Signs Orders for Rs 2/kg Rice and Free Color TVs
newsItems.description: In his first cabinet meeting, Chief Minister M. Karunanidhi signs files to implement the Rs 2 per kg rice scheme and orders tenders for the first phase of free color TVs.

reactionOptions.text: Approve global tenders for television procurement and increase state grain allocations at ration shops.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition files cases alleging favoritism in selecting electronic suppliers.

reactionOptions.text: Expose procurement details, demanding an investigation into why public money is spent on commercial imports.

risk.badOutcome: Rural families express anger at the opposition for trying to stall the TV delivery.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that the TVs be assembled within the state to create employment for engineering graduates.

risk.badOutcome: Supplier companies state that setting up local plants will take over a year.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Hogenakkal Water Supply Project Survey Sparks Row
newsItems.description: The Tamil Nadu government begins land and hydrological surveys for the Hogenakkal Drinking Water Project in Dharmapuri. Karnataka politicians raise strong objections.

reactionOptions.text: Assert that Hogenakkal falls within state borders and continue the survey under police protection.

risk.badOutcome: Karnataka activists stage protests near the border, disrupting commercial transport.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the state government lead an all-party delegation to the Prime Minister to secure central protection.

risk.badOutcome: PMO delays meeting schedule due to ongoing Parliament session.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint technical water commission with Karnataka representatives to verify water levels at Hogenakkal.

risk.badOutcome: Karnataka government boycotts the commission, continuing its protests.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Congress and DMK Negotiate Local Body Seat Sharing
newsItems.description: Tensions rise within the ruling alliance as local Congress committees demand a higher share of mayoral and ward seats for the upcoming civic body polls.

reactionOptions.text: Concede the Madurai and Coimbatore mayoral candidacies to allies to preserve the front's stability.

risk.badOutcome: Local DMK district secretaries rebel, filing independent nominations in key wards.

reactionOptions.text: Refuse to yield seats, stating that the party's own cadres are best placed to win municipal posts.

risk.badOutcome: Allies run separate campaigns in several wards, splitting the alliance vote.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint civic manifesto focusing on city garbage disposal and sewer upgrades to unite both cadres.

risk.badOutcome: Seat disputes overshadow the manifesto, reducing public interest.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: First Phase of Free Color TV Distribution Launched
newsItems.description: The Chief Minister launches the first phase of Free Color TV distribution at a function in Chennai, handing over sets to poor families in selected slums.

reactionOptions.text: Expand the distribution drive to rural panchayats, prioritizing low-income households first.

risk.badOutcome: Villages report supply delays due to manufacturing backlogs, drawing local complaints.

reactionOptions.text: Expose reports of malfunctioning TV sets in some centers, accusing the government of buying cheap quality.

risk.badOutcome: Suppliers quickly replace the damaged units, weakening the opposition critique.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up local cooperative electronics repair centers to handle warranty issues and create jobs.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of trained technicians delays the repair center rollout.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Violence and Rigging Allegations Mar Chennai Civic Polls
newsItems.description: The Chennai Corporation elections witness clashes, booth-capturing allegations, and police lathi-charges. Opposition AIADMK demands re-polling in all wards.

reactionOptions.text: Reject the systemic rigging claims but order re-polling in 12 specific booths where logs show anomalies.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition files high court petition and launches a state-wide dharna over democracy murder.

reactionOptions.text: Organize a mass dharna outside the State Election Commission, demanding the arrest of the commissioner.

risk.badOutcome: High court stays the protest, forcing cadres to disperse with minor injuries.

reactionOptions.text: Propose complete CCTV recording and central paramilitary force deployment for all re-polling wards.

risk.badOutcome: Administrative delays in hiring cameras push back the poll dates.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Cauvery Tribunal Draft Allocation Triggers Farmer Concerns
newsItems.description: The draft findings of the Cauvery Water Disputes Tribunal are circulated. Delta farmer groups express concerns that the proposed monthly allocations are insufficient.

reactionOptions.text: Submit an official revision memorandum to the tribunal demanding guaranteed shares during distressed years.

risk.badOutcome: Tribunal delays reviewing the memorandum, keeping the draft unchanged.

reactionOptions.text: Lead farmer dharnas outside central water offices, demanding the UPA government guarantee the state's water share.

risk.badOutcome: Water department officials claim the allocations are final, stalling discussions.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up an inter-state non-political council of farmers to negotiate water sharing directly.

risk.badOutcome: Neighboring state farmer unions boycott the council, citing regional interests.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: State Government Waives Off Cooperative Farm Loans
newsItems.description: The state government announces a complete waiver of agricultural loans availed by small and marginal farmers from cooperative banks, totaling over Rs 3,000 crore.

reactionOptions.text: Release matching state budget funds to cooperative banks to restore their lending capacities immediately.

risk.badOutcome: Commercial banks refuse to extend new credits, claiming cooperative debts are risky.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that nationalized commercial banks waive off all agricultural debts too, calling the state scheme partial.

risk.badOutcome: Union Finance Ministry rejects the demand, citing national credit norms.

reactionOptions.text: Propose integrating the waiver with a compulsory crop insurance scheme to prevent future defaults.

risk.badOutcome: Insurance enrollment processes are slow, leaving many farmers uncovered.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Uniform Education System Feasibility Committee Set Up
newsItems.description: The government appoints a high-level committee to study the feasibility of implementing a Uniform System of School Education (Samacheer Kalvi) across all schools.

reactionOptions.text: Approve the committee's recommendation to unify State Board, Matriculation, and Anglo-Indian syllabi.

risk.badOutcome: Private school managements protest, claiming the uniform syllabus dilutes quality.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that private schools retain their syllabus choices, accusing the government of ideological control.

risk.badOutcome: Parent-teacher associations support the uniform syllabus, reducing opposition impact.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a phased introduction starting with primary classes to allow teacher training and book printings.

risk.badOutcome: Printing delays leave several schools without new books in the first term.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Cauvery Water Tribunal Delivers Final Verdict
newsItems.description: The Cauvery Water Disputes Tribunal delivers its final verdict, allocating 419 tmc ft of water annually to Tamil Nadu. Karnataka plans to appeal in the Supreme Court.

reactionOptions.text: Welcome the verdict and demand the central government immediately gazette the order to make it legally binding.

risk.badOutcome: Central government delays gazetting due to upcoming elections in Karnataka.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the central government's delay in gazetting the verdict and hold black-flag protests.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party accuses the opposition of trying to damage relations with the Centre.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint basin audit board representing both states to monitor water storage levels weekly.

risk.badOutcome: Karnataka officials decline the audit board, citing sovereignty over their dams.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Chennai Metro Rail Proposal Sent for Central Clearance
newsItems.description: The state government submits the detailed project report (DPR) for Phase I of the Chennai Metro Rail Project, seeking cabinet approvals and financial tie-ups.

reactionOptions.text: Negotiate soft loans with international funding agencies (like JICA) to secure low-interest capital.

risk.badOutcome: Negotiation terms demand matching land acquisition speed, creating local pressures.

reactionOptions.text: Expose maps of proposed corridors, claiming that hundreds of heritage commercial buildings will be demolished.

risk.badOutcome: Metro planners alter designs to tunnel underground, neutralizing the critique.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a cheaper Monorail extension to connect far-off suburbs with the main Metro stations.

risk.badOutcome: Technical coordination between Metro and Monorail designs delays approvals.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: NLC Contract Workers Strike Over Regularization
newsItems.description: Over 10,000 contract workers at the Neyveli Lignite Corporation (NLC) launch an indefinite strike, demanding job regularization and equal pay as permanent staff.

reactionOptions.text: Appoint a state labor team to mediate between union leaders and the central coal ministry to find a compromise.

risk.badOutcome: Coal ministry rejects immediate regularization, leading to resumption of strike.

reactionOptions.text: Join the strike in solidarity, blockading NLC gates and demanding that the CM intervene personally.

risk.badOutcome: Power generation drops, causing brief power cuts across northern districts.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a phased induction policy to regularize 1,000 workers annually based on seniority.

risk.badOutcome: Radical sections of the union reject the phased model, demanding total relief.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Madurai Office of 'Dinakaran' Newspaper Attacked by Protestors
newsItems.description: A violent crowd attacks the Madurai office of the 'Dinakaran' newspaper after it publishes a survey showing M.K. Stalin as the preferred successor to the CM over Alagiri.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the violence, order the immediate arrest of the attackers, and refer the case to the CBI.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling family divisions spill into the open, causing administrative confusion.

reactionOptions.text: Accuse the government of failing to protect the press and demand the resignation of the minister handling police.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party points to the arrests made, calling the demand political posturing.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a state bill to provide special security and legal protections to media houses during political rows.

risk.badOutcome: Journalist unions call the bill a token gesture, demanding wider law and order action.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Launches Free Gas Stoves and LPG Scheme
newsItems.description: The state government launches a major welfare scheme to distribute free gas stoves and provide LPG cylinder connections to rural women to replace wood-fired stoves.

reactionOptions.text: Expedite stove procurements and setup local cylinder distribution agencies in rural hubs.

risk.badOutcome: Refill cylinder prices rise nationally, making it hard for poor families to buy refills.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the government increase the cheap kerosene ration instead, calling gas connections a burden.

risk.badOutcome: Rural women welcome the clean stoves, ignoring the kerosene arguments.

reactionOptions.text: Propose matching subsidies for community biogas units in cattle-rearing villages as a cheaper alternative.

risk.badOutcome: Maintenance issues in community bio-gas tanks keep them mostly offline.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Land Acquisition Protests Erupt for Nanguneri SEZ
newsItems.description: Farmers and local activists in Tirunelveli district protest against the acquisition of agricultural land for the proposed Nanguneri Special Economic Zone.

reactionOptions.text: Announce a 30% hike in land compensation and guarantee one job per affected family in the incoming industries.

risk.badOutcome: Private developers object to job guarantees, delaying investment commitments.

reactionOptions.text: Join the farmers' rally, demanding that the SEZ be relocated to barren land in adjacent blocks.

risk.badOutcome: Local builders and contractors accuse the party of blocking development in Nellai.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a lease model where farmers retain land ownership and receive monthly rentals from the industries.

risk.badOutcome: Legal structures for long-term corporate leases are delayed in assembly.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Sethusamudram Shipping Canal Controversy Escalates
newsItems.description: The Sethusamudram Shipping Canal Project sparks debate. Conservative groups and opposition parties claim that dredging the canal will damage the Ram Setu bridge.

reactionOptions.text: Defend the project, stating that the canal will reduce shipping times and bring massive growth to southern ports.

risk.badOutcome: Conservative organizations launch state-wide human chain protests and blockades.

reactionOptions.text: Oppose the current dredging route, demanding the project be aligned to bypass the Ram Setu structure.

risk.badOutcome: Industrial associations criticize the opposition for stalling crucial shipping infra.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an independent commission of marine archeologists to study the structure before dredging proceeds.

risk.badOutcome: Supreme Court halts dredging pending study submissions, delaying the project.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: CM Karunanidhi's Remarks on Lord Ram Cause Row
newsItems.description: Chief Minister Karunanidhi makes controversial remarks questioning the historical existence of Lord Ram during a speech on the Sethusamudram project, drawing protests.

reactionOptions.text: Defend the statements as consistent with the rationalist Dravidian ideology of social reformer Periyar.

risk.badOutcome: Conservative bodies call for a state-wide bandh, leading to minor clashes in temple towns.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the remarks as an insult to millions of believers and demand an immediate public apology from the CM.

risk.badOutcome: Rationalist forums organize counter-rallies, accusing the opposition of communalism.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that both sides move past theological arguments and focus on the project's financial feasibility.

risk.badOutcome: Media ignores the financial arguments, keeping focus on the religious row.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Supreme Court Stays Sethusamudram Dredging at Ram Setu
newsItems.description: The Supreme Court issues an interim stay on dredging activities at the Ram Setu site, responding to petitions regarding environmental impact and cultural heritage.

reactionOptions.text: Comply with the stay order, directing state geologists to prepare detailed data for the next hearing.

risk.badOutcome: Coalition allies express frustration over the judicial delay, creating minor friction.

reactionOptions.text: Hail the stay as a victory for cultural heritage and demand a permanent cancellation of the current route.

risk.badOutcome: Port worker unions complain that the stay delays shipping sector employment.

reactionOptions.text: Propose adding independent environmentalists to the state legal team to present a balanced view.

risk.badOutcome: Selecting acceptable panel members delays the court affidavit submission.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: DMK Youth Wing Conference in Nellai Showcases Stalin's Leadership
newsItems.description: A massive DMK Youth Wing Conference is held in Tirunelveli (Nellai). The event focuses on M.K. Stalin's leadership, signaling a succession transition within the party.

reactionOptions.text: Approve the elevation of several youth wing members to district administrative posts to revitalize the party.

risk.badOutcome: Older senior leaders express resentment over being sidelined by younger cadres.

reactionOptions.text: Hold public campaigns criticizing the transition as dynastic politics, calling it a disregard for democratic merit.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party releases lists of opposition family members in politics, neutralizing impact.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a legislative resolution encouraging all parties to reserve 20% of assembly tickets for youth candidates.

risk.badOutcome: Rival parties ignore the proposal, calling it a PR distraction.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Demolition Notices Issued to ECR Resorts for CRZ Violations
newsItems.description: The Coastal Zone Management Authority issues demolition notices to several luxury resorts and private villas along the East Coast Road (ECR) for violating environmental rules.

reactionOptions.text: Enforce the notices strictly, stating that no commercial violations will be tolerated on coastal zones.

risk.badOutcome: Resort owners association files a court appeal, securing a stay on notices.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state focus demolitions on major corporate builders rather than small local fishing homes.

risk.badOutcome: Fishermen unions state that their residential zones are unaffected, dulling the issue.

reactionOptions.text: Propose drafting clear eco-tourism guidelines that allow low-impact resorts while protecting the coast.

risk.badOutcome: Drafting guidelines delays enforcement, allowing violations to remain active.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Introduces 3.5% Muslim Sub-Quota
newsItems.description: The state government passes an ordinance providing a 3.5% sub-quota for Muslims within the Backward Classes reservation pool in government jobs and education.

reactionOptions.text: Implement the sub-quota immediately for the current academic admissions cycle.

risk.badOutcome: Other backward communities express concern about reduced general BC seats.

reactionOptions.text: Demand similar sub-quotas for other neglected backward communities, calling the policy selective.

risk.badOutcome: Minority organizations accuse the party of seeking to block their benefits.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a fresh socio-economic survey to scientificially determine backwardness prior to splitting quotas.

risk.badOutcome: Survey execution delays recruitment processes, causing anger among job seekers.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Assembly Resolution Urges Centre to Complete Shipping Canal
newsItems.description: The Tamil Nadu Assembly passes a resolution urging the central government to complete the Sethusamudram Shipping Canal Project on the current alignment without delays.

reactionOptions.text: Send a ministerial delegation to Delhi to submit the resolution to the Prime Minister.

risk.badOutcome: Central ministries request patience citing ongoing Supreme Court stay hearings.

reactionOptions.text: Boycott the assembly vote, claiming the resolution disregards the religious sentiments of millions.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party uses the boycott to label the opposition as anti-development.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a bipartisan committee of legal experts to draft a joint affidavit for the Supreme Court.

risk.badOutcome: Disagreements over Ram Setu's status prevent the committee from drafting the affidavit.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Foundation Stone Laid for Hogenakkal Drinking Water Project
newsItems.description: Chief Minister Karunanidhi lays the foundation stone for the Hogenakkal Drinking Water Project. Protests break out in Karnataka, with calls to boycott Tamil goods.

reactionOptions.text: Declare that the project is vital for the fluorosis-affected districts of Dharmapuri and will proceed on schedule.

risk.badOutcome: Border transport links are shut down by protestors, causing trade losses.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the attacks on Tamil establishments in Bangalore and demand central security for border areas.

risk.badOutcome: Union Home ministry urges both states to show restraint, delaying central force dispatch.

reactionOptions.text: Propose immediate tripartite talks between the two state CMs and the central water resources minister.

risk.badOutcome: Karnataka government declines talks until after their state elections.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: CM Temporarily Puts Hogenakkal Water Project on Hold
newsItems.description: Chief Minister Karunanidhi announces that the Hogenakkal project will be put on temporary hold until elections in Karnataka are completed, to avoid provoking communal rows.

reactionOptions.text: Defend the decision as responsible statesmanship to protect Tamil lives and properties in Karnataka.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition labels the decision as surrender, staging protests in Dharmapuri.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the deferral as a cowardly surrender of state rights, demanding immediate resumption of work.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party points out that the pause is brief, neutralizing some criticism.

reactionOptions.text: Propose constructing internal distribution pipes within Dharmapuri first while waiting for border clearances.

risk.badOutcome: Contractors refuse to lay pipes without source reservoir clarity, delaying work.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Farmers in Erode Protest Noyyal River Pollution
newsItems.description: Farmer organizations block local highways in Erode, protesting against the discharge of untreated chemical effluents from Tiruppur dyeing units into the Noyyal river.

reactionOptions.text: Order state pollution board to inspect the dyeing units and seal those without zero liquid discharge (ZLD) systems.

risk.badOutcome: Dyeing owners threaten layoffs, leading to protests by textile labor unions.

reactionOptions.text: Lead a farmers' march to the state pollution control board office demanding permanent closure of violating units.

risk.badOutcome: Textile export associations warning of order cancellations and job losses receives press.

reactionOptions.text: Propose state subsidies for establishing Common Effluent Treatment Plants (CETPs) in textile zones.

risk.badOutcome: Construction of CETPs is delayed by land dispute clearances.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Committee Submits Local Body Decentralization Report
newsItems.description: The state committee on administrative reforms submits its report, recommending the transfer of health and primary education budgets directly to municipal councils.

reactionOptions.text: Accept the report recommendations and initiate legislative bills to transfer primary health funds to corporations.

risk.badOutcome: State department officers association protests their loss of administrative oversight.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the government first conduct a financial audit of municipal expenditures before transferring new budgets.

risk.badOutcome: Mayors accuse the opposition of blocking local municipal empowerment.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a pilot run in two corporations to test the budget transfer mechanism before a state-wide rollout.

risk.badOutcome: Bureaucratic delays keep the pilot guidelines stuck in department files.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: PMK Withdraws Support from DMK minority State Government
newsItems.description: The Pattali Makkal Katchi (PMK) announces the withdrawal of its support from the DMK minority state government, citing differences over reservation and local issues.

reactionOptions.text: Reassure that the government remains stable with the backing of Congress and Left parties, dismissing the pullout.

risk.badOutcome: Left parties express minor differences over central fuel price hikes, increasing tension.

reactionOptions.text: Label the government unstable and demand the Governor order an immediate floor test in the assembly.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party coordinates with Congress, passing a voice vote easily during budget.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a new legislative coordination charter to discuss bill amendments with all parties before voting.

risk.badOutcome: Rival parties reject the charter, focusing on seat bargaining.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Union Cabinet Approves Chennai Metro Rail Project Funding
newsItems.description: The Union Cabinet formally approves the Chennai Metro Rail Project Phase I, committing to share 50% of the project cost under joint ownership with the state.

reactionOptions.text: Commence the route demarcation and issue international tenders for tunneling and coach imports.

risk.badOutcome: Shopkeepers in historical commercial zones launch protests against land acquisition.

reactionOptions.text: Support the shopkeepers, demanding that the government pay 300% market rate compensation for acquisitions.

risk.badOutcome: Transit advocates write columns criticizing the opposition for delaying public transport.

reactionOptions.text: Propose modifying the design to use elevated corridors instead of underground tunnels to reduce acquisition costs.

risk.badOutcome: Metro planners reject elevated design in old heritage zones due to narrow roads.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Global Financial Crisis Hits Sriperumbudur Automobile Hub
newsItems.description: The global financial downturn leads to export order cancellations, hitting the automobile and electronic manufacturing hubs in Sriperumbudur and Oragadam.

reactionOptions.text: Announce an emergency state incentive package reducing electricity duty for export-oriented factories.

risk.badOutcome: Revenue department warns of tax collections falling below state targets.

reactionOptions.text: Expose job loss statistics, demanding the state government ban lay-offs of contract assembly staff.

risk.badOutcome: Manufacturing associations claim strict ban will force factory closures.

reactionOptions.text: Propose state-backed re-skilling grants for laid-off youth to transition to food processing industries.

risk.badOutcome: Coordination delays with technical colleges keep the grants offline.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Assembly Resolution Demands Indian Intervention for SL Ceasefire
newsItems.description: As the Sri Lankan Civil War intensifies, the Tamil Nadu Assembly passes a resolution urging the central government to halt military assistance and demand a ceasefire.

reactionOptions.text: Threaten that state MPs will resign from the central cabinet if India does not enforce an immediate ceasefire.

risk.badOutcome: Central ministries state that foreign policy cannot be dictated by state-level demands.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the MP resignation threats as an empty drama and demand the ruling party pull out of UPA cabinet today.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party releases correspondence proving active lobbying with the PM.

reactionOptions.text: Propose sending a joint all-party relief consignment of food and medicines to northern Sri Lanka under UN flags.

risk.badOutcome: Sri Lankan government delays clearance for the consignment ship at Colombo.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: All-Party Rally Demands Halt of Indian Military Aid to SL
newsItems.description: A massive all-party rally is organized in Chennai. Leaders demand the UPA government immediately stop radar and logistics support to the Sri Lankan army.

reactionOptions.text: Convey the unanimous views of the rally to the Prime Minister during a personal cabinet meet in Delhi.

risk.badOutcome: Defense analysts write columns asserting that India cannot compromise regional security interests.

reactionOptions.text: Lead student wing picketing of central government passport and railway offices across the state.

risk.badOutcome: Police arrests several student protestors, leading to minor injuries and local disruption.

reactionOptions.text: Propose establishing a special state fund to build permanent concrete housing for Sri Lankan Tamil refugees in camps.

risk.badOutcome: Central intelligence agencies raise security objections over permanent camp housing.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: State-wide Human Chain Protest in Support of SL Tamils
newsItems.description: Thousands of citizens, students, and political workers form a massive human chain across the state capital in support of civilian protection in the Sri Lankan war zone.

reactionOptions.text: Direct all cabinet ministers to participate in the human chain to express solidarity with the civilian struggle.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition claims the ministers are protest-acting while retaining central cabinet posts.

reactionOptions.text: Expose state budget items funding central police academies, accusing the govt of double standards on security.

risk.badOutcome: Voters find the comparison far-fetched, ignoring the double standard argument.

reactionOptions.text: Propose sending an unofficial delegation of cultural leaders to the UN Human Rights Council in Geneva.

risk.badOutcome: Visa and diplomatic passport clearance delays prevent the delegation from traveling.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Sri Lankan Civil War Casualties Trigger Protests in State
newsItems.description: Reports of high civilian casualties in the Mullaitivu region of Sri Lanka trigger intense protests across Tamil Nadu, with several self-immolation incidents.

reactionOptions.text: Appeal for public calm, announce state compensation for self-immolation families, and send emergency relief materials.

risk.badOutcome: Protestors call the compensation an insult, storming government offices in central districts.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the ruling UPA for complicity in the war and demand immediate withdrawal of all support to the Centre.

risk.badOutcome: Police makes preventive arrests of key leaders under assembly guidelines.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a unanimous assembly resolution demanding that international observers be allowed into Sri Lankan refugee camps.

risk.badOutcome: Central government declines to forward the resolution, citing diplomatic limits.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: PMK and MDMK Join AIADMK Alliance for Lok Sabha Polls
newsItems.description: Ahead of the general elections, the PMK and MDMK formally join the AIADMK-led alliance, creating a strong consolidated front against the DMK-Congress camp.

reactionOptions.text: Launch a joint campaign, highlighting the ruling front's failures on power cuts and the Sri Lankan issue.

risk.badOutcome: Cadres of allied parties dispute local campaign budgets, causing minor friction.

reactionOptions.text: Dismiss the alliance as a grouping of parties that lost the previous Lok Sabha contests, expressing confidence.

risk.badOutcome: Combined voting percentage of the new front looks high in initial poll logs.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a growth charter detailing how the regional alliance will secure federal funds for state projects.

risk.badOutcome: National parties ignore the regional charter, focusing on national stability themes.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Violent Clash in Madras High Court Premises
newsItems.description: A violent clash erupts between advocates protesting the Sri Lankan issue and state police inside the Madras High Court premises. Vehicles are set on fire.

reactionOptions.text: Order a judicial inquiry by a retired judge and suspend the police officers who ordered the lathi-charge.

risk.badOutcome: Police officers association protests, claiming they were acting under orders to clear court entries.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the immediate resignation of the Home Secretary and organize lawyer boycotts in all district courts.

risk.badOutcome: Litigants face delays due to court boycotts, generating public criticism.

reactionOptions.text: Propose establishing a separate court security police division under the direct control of the High Court Registrar.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of immediate funds delays training of the special security division.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: CM Karunanidhi Goes on Sudden Hunger Strike for SL Ceasefire
newsItems.description: Chief Minister Karunanidhi launches a sudden hunger strike at the Anna Memorial in Chennai, demanding the Sri Lankan government declare an immediate ceasefire.

reactionOptions.text: Terminate the hunger strike after six hours, claiming the central government received assurances of ceasefire.

risk.badOutcome: Reports emerge that the war has not stopped, leading to opposition mockery of the fast-strike drama.

reactionOptions.text: Label the hunger strike as a brief, political charade designed to fool voters before next week's Lok Sabha polling.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party cadres stage blockades, accusing the opposition of anti-Tamil bias.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint all-party delegation to lobby the foreign embassies in Delhi for diplomatic intervention.

risk.badOutcome: Embassies refuse to meet the state delegation citing international protocol rules.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: UPA Alliance Wins 27 Seats in Tamil Nadu; SL War Ends
newsItems.description: The general election results are declared. The DMK-Congress UPA alliance wins 27 seats in Tamil Nadu. In Sri Lanka, the military defeats the LTTE, ending the civil war.

reactionOptions.text: Celebrate the victory as voter endorsement of welfare schemes and negotiate ministerial berths in the new cabinet.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition claims the win was secured by cash distribution and state machinery misuse.

reactionOptions.text: Release a report detailing systematic voter bribe payments in multiple districts and demand re-polls.

risk.badOutcome: EC validates the results, stating that opposition charges lack specific proof logs.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a national charter to assist the repatriation and rehabilitation of displaced Sri Lankan Tamils.

risk.badOutcome: Sri Lankan government delays visa approvals for Indian rehabilitation teams.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: M.K. Stalin Appointed Deputy Chief Minister
newsItems.description: Chief Minister Karunanidhi appoints Local Administration Minister M.K. Stalin as the Deputy Chief Minister, officially handing him charge of Home and General Administration.

reactionOptions.text: Consolidate the state administration under the new Deputy CM, launching city development monitoring drives.

risk.badOutcome: Madurai district secretary Alagiri's supporters express dissent, delaying local project works.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the appointment, calling it the formalization of dynastic rule in Tamil Nadu and bypassing senior leaders.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party releases list of senior cabinet promotions, proving merit-based appointments.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a bipartisan assembly committee to discuss urban governance and local body finance devolution.

risk.badOutcome: Rival parties boycott committee meetings, stalling the devolution draft.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Kalaignar Insurance Scheme for Poor Families Launched
newsItems.description: The state government launches the Kalaignar Insurance Scheme, a cashless health insurance program providing up to Rs 1 lakh for life-saving treatments for poor families.

reactionOptions.text: Organize school-level enrollment camps and issue smartcards to speed up health coverage.

risk.badOutcome: Private hospitals report billing delay disputes, temporarily turning away patients.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that government funds be spent upgrading state hospitals instead of paying premiums to private insurers.

risk.badOutcome: Voters welcome the cashless private hospital options, ignoring the critique.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint panel of medical experts and MLAs to monitor the quality of surgeries performed under the scheme.

risk.badOutcome: Hospital lobbies delay panel inspections citing patient privacy rules.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: High Court Orders Closure of Polluting Tiruppur Dyeing Units
newsItems.description: The Madras High Court directs the closure of all dyeing units in Tiruppur that fail to achieve Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) of effluents into the Noyyal river.

reactionOptions.text: Implement the closures strictly and announce state loans for setting up advanced ZLD plants.

risk.badOutcome: Textile export unions declare a strike, leading to mass lay-offs of garment workers.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state government provide immediate monthly cash assistance to laid-off garment workers.

risk.badOutcome: Finance department claims budget limits, refusing to issue cash payouts.

reactionOptions.text: Propose soft loans for displaced workers to shift to organic farming or non-polluting manufacturing.

risk.badOutcome: Workers show low interest in organic farming loans, leaving funds unused.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: 108 Emergency Ambulance Service Network Expanded
newsItems.description: The state health department announces the deployment of 200 new high-tech ambulances to expand the 108 Emergency service in rural and hilly districts.

reactionOptions.text: Fast-track GPS integration in dispatch rooms to reduce response times in remote villages.

risk.badOutcome: Cellular network issues in hilly zones cause dispatch delays, drawing local press.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the government build ICU facilities in taluk hospitals first, calling ambulances transport vans.

risk.badOutcome: Public welcomes the quick ambulance response times, ignoring the critique.

reactionOptions.text: Propose training village youth as certified first responders to manage medical emergencies before ambulances arrive.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of trainer doctors limits the number of youth certified in the first quarter.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Row Over Land Acquisition for Coimbatore Airport Expansion
newsItems.description: Landowners and farmer groups in Coimbatore block airport access roads, protesting against the state's land acquisition rates for the runway expansion project.

reactionOptions.text: Raise compensation rates and offer alternative commercial shop plots to affected families.

risk.badOutcome: Re-routing access roads delays expansion works by over six months.

reactionOptions.text: Join the farmers' protest, demanding that the runway expansion be redesigned to avoid agricultural fields.

risk.badOutcome: Chamber of commerce accuses the party of blocking industrial growth in Western TN.

reactionOptions.text: Propose offering equity shares in the airport corporation to landowners in exchange for their plots.

risk.badOutcome: Civil aviation ministry raises legal objections to state-offered private equity.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Controversy Over Private Medical College Fee Regulations
newsItems.description: A government-appointed committee fixes fee structures for private medical colleges. Managements protest, claiming the fees do not cover operational costs.

reactionOptions.text: Enforce the fixed fee structures strictly, warning of license cancellation for colleges charging capitation fees.

risk.badOutcome: Managements association shuts down admission counters in protest, stalling selection.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state government immediately take over colleges that refuse to comply with fee limits.

risk.badOutcome: Legal advisors state that arbitrary takeover violates property rights, stalling action.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a differential fee model where high-income students pay full costs to subsidize poor students.

risk.badOutcome: Private colleges challenge the model in court, securing an interim stay.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: AIADMK Boycotts Assembly Sessions Alleging Speaker Bias
newsItems.description: Opposition party AIADMK announces a boycott of the winter assembly sessions, alleging that the Speaker is biased and does not allocate sufficient debate time.

reactionOptions.text: Proceed with passing bills on schedule, stating that the opposition is running away from debates.

risk.badOutcome: Assembly debates look one-sided, drawing critical editorials on democratic norms.

reactionOptions.text: Hold parallel mock assembly sessions on the streets, highlighting crucial issues like power cuts.

risk.badOutcome: Police detains MLAs for holding unauthorized public gatherings.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that the Speaker convene a special reconciliation meeting to draft new debate time allocations.

risk.badOutcome: Rival leaders refuse to attend, leaving the boycott active.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: DMK Candidate Wins Pennagaram Assembly By-Election
newsItems.description: The DMK candidate wins the Pennagaram assembly by-election, defeating the PMK and AIADMK. The opposition alleges widespread distribution of cash to voters.

reactionOptions.text: Celebrate the victory as public endorsement of the deputy CM's development and welfare programs.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition files compilation of cash distribution videos with the Election Commission.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the EC disqualify the candidate and order fresh polling under central police supervision.

risk.badOutcome: EC holds inquiry but validates results citing lack of direct link to candidate.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that the EC use real-time bank monitoring of all candidate accounts during by-elections.

risk.badOutcome: EC cites lack of banking integration in rural zones, stalling the proposal.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: PMK Calls for Non-Aligned Third Front in State Politics
newsItems.description: PMK founder S. Ramadoss calls on smaller regional parties and caste organizations to form a non-aligned third front to break the DMK-AIADMK duopoly.

reactionOptions.text: Ignore the call publicly, but initiate private bilateral discussions with key constituent parties.

risk.badOutcome: Allies leak details of private talks, causing embarrassment and minor friction.

reactionOptions.text: Warn smaller parties that a third front only benefits the ruling regime by splitting anti-incumbency votes.

risk.badOutcome: PMK leaders criticize the opposition for being arrogant and dismissive of smaller groups.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a coalition governance seminar to discuss how multi-party fronts can run stable administrations.

risk.badOutcome: Low turnout at the seminar limits its political impact.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: New Legislative Assembly-Secretariat Complex Inaugurated
newsItems.description: Prime Minister Manmohan Singh inaugurates the grand new Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly-Secretariat complex at Government Estate in Chennai, built under the DMK regime.

reactionOptions.text: Hold the budget assembly session in the new hall, highlighting it as a modern symbol of state pride.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition boycotted the opening, alleging high project cost overruns and poor ventilation.

reactionOptions.text: Expose budget files of the project, pledging to convert the building into a public hospital if voted to power.

risk.badOutcome: Architectural associations criticize the pledge to shift functions as wasteful.

reactionOptions.text: Propose organizing guided tours for school students to view the eco-friendly design elements of the complex.

risk.badOutcome: Security departments restrict tourist access citing security concerns.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Uniform Education System Rolled Out in State Schools
newsItems.description: The state education department begins rolling out the Uniform Education System (Samacheer Kalvi) for classes 1 and 6, using unified textbooks printed by the government.

reactionOptions.text: Distribute free uniform textbooks to all government school children on the first day of the term.

risk.badOutcome: Private schools challenge the textbook content in court, seeking permission to use extra guides.

reactionOptions.text: Expose instances of historical errors and praise of political leaders in the new textbooks, demanding revisions.

risk.badOutcome: Education department issues corrigendum slips, neutralizing the textbook controversy.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a curriculum review board of university professors to screen textbooks annually before printing.

risk.badOutcome: Board formation is delayed, leaving current errors uncorrected in classes.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: 100 MLD Minjur Desalination Plant Starts Operations
newsItems.description: The Minjur Desalination Plant starts full operations, supplying 100 million litres of water daily to industrial units and residential areas in North Chennai.

reactionOptions.text: Inaugurate pipeline extensions connecting the plant directly to North Chennai municipal housing units.

risk.badOutcome: Minor pipeline leaks in industrial zones delay water delivery to homes.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a CAG audit of the water purchase agreement, claiming the state pays high prices to the private plant operator.

risk.badOutcome: Public welcomes the regular water supply, ignoring the tariff audit demand.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up a dedicated solar farm near the plant to reduce high grid power consumption costs.

risk.badOutcome: Land acquisition issues for the solar farm delay panel setup.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: World Classical Tamil Conference Held in Coimbatore
newsItems.description: The state government hosts the historic World Classical Tamil Conference (Semmozhi Conference) in Coimbatore, celebrating Tamil language, culture, and literature.

reactionOptions.text: Advertise the conference globally, showcasing the Dravidian regime's contribution to securing classical language status.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition boycotts the event, calling it a self-aggrandizing show for the CM's family.

reactionOptions.text: Criticize the massive expenditure on the conference while Western districts face daily four-hour power cuts.

risk.badOutcome: Tamil scholars criticize the party for politicizing a linguistic celebration.

reactionOptions.text: Propose establishing 100 permanent international research fellowships for Tamil studies in state universities.

risk.badOutcome: University senate approval delays prevent early fellowship admissions.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: 2G Spectrum Allocation Controversy Targets DMK Minister
newsItems.description: Opposition parties target the DMK, alleging that Union Telecom Minister A. Raja bypassed guidelines in allocating 2G spectrum licenses, causing huge loss to exchequer.

reactionOptions.text: Defend the policy in media, arguing that the pricing ensured cheap telephone access for millions of rural poor.

risk.badOutcome: National audit reports leaks detail massive discrepancies, damaging state govt image.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the immediate resignation and arrest of the minister, holding state-wide protests over corruption.

risk.badOutcome: Police detains protestors, leading to localized clashes near party offices.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) inquiry to review the entire spectrum pricing history since 1999.

risk.badOutcome: Parliamentary debate deadlocks delay JPC formation by several months.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Farmers Protest High-Voltage Lines on Cropland in West
newsItems.description: Farmer groups in Tiruppur and Coimbatore protest against the installation of high-voltage power transmission lines across agricultural lands, demanding underground cables.

reactionOptions.text: Offer double compensation for land easement rights and guarantee subsidized power for affected farms.

risk.badOutcome: State power utility states underground cabling is technically impossible for high voltage, stalling talks.

reactionOptions.text: Join the farmers' dharna demanding the line route be shifted along state highways to avoid cropland.

risk.badOutcome: Utility security calls police to clear transmission towers, leading to minor clashes.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an independent expert panel to audit radiation and crop yield safety under transmission lines.

risk.badOutcome: Panel inspections take months, keeping project construction on hold.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: High Court Stays Uniform Syllabus for Private Schools
newsItems.description: The Madras High Court issues a stay on the implementation of the uniform syllabus for private matriculation schools, citing lack of consultation on elective courses.

reactionOptions.text: File an immediate appeal in the Supreme Court to lift the stay, asserting the state's right to unify school syllabus.

risk.badOutcome: SC delays hearings, leaving parents and teachers confused over which books to study.

reactionOptions.text: Hail the stay and demand the government revise the textbooks to include standard international science sections.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party accuses the opposition of siding with private school lobbies.

reactionOptions.text: Propose allowing schools to choose between state books and matriculation courses for the current academic term.

risk.badOutcome: Double examinations confusion arises, frustrating students.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Supreme Court Upholds Samacheer Kalvi Implementation
newsItems.description: The Supreme Court sets aside the High Court stay, upholding the implementation of Samacheer Kalvi. The court orders all schools to use the uniform syllabus.

reactionOptions.text: Direct all district educational officers to inspect schools and distribute free study guides for students.

risk.badOutcome: Private schools lobby for extra school hours to teach CBSE content.

reactionOptions.text: Comply with the order but demand a committee remove sections praising local political figures from history books.

risk.badOutcome: Educational department delays book changes, keeping current prints active.

reactionOptions.text: Propose state training grants for teachers to adapt to the new uniform curriculum formats.

risk.badOutcome: Training center schedules overlap with exam terms, reducing attendance.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Union Telecom Minister A. Raja Resigns Over CAG Report
newsItems.description: Following the tabling of the CAG report detailing an estimated loss of Rs 1.76 lakh crore in 2G spectrum allocations, Telecom Minister A. Raja resigns from cabinet.

reactionOptions.text: Accept the resignation, stating that the minister resigned to protect parliament values, and promise full cooperation.

risk.badOutcome: CBI carries out raids on state capital trust offices, generating critical news headlines.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the Governor dismiss the state minority government, calling it a regime built on spectrum scam funds.

risk.badOutcome: Governor states the assembly majority remains stable, rejecting the petition.

reactionOptions.text: Propose establishing a state Lokayukta (ombudsman) with independent powers to scan MLA assets.

risk.badOutcome: Drafting the ombudsman bill is delayed by assembly committee debates.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Monsoon Floods Damage Crops in Delta Districts
newsItems.description: Heavy northeast monsoon rains submerge thousands of acres of standing paddy crops in Thanjavur and Tiruvarur. Farmers demand emergency relief.

reactionOptions.text: Announce an emergency compensation of Rs 10,000 per damaged acre and waive current crop loans.

risk.badOutcome: Revenue surveyors take weeks to measure damaged plots, stalling payouts.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the central government send a team and release an emergency relief package of Rs 500 crore.

risk.badOutcome: Central team delays visit, leaving farmers to wait for relief.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a long-term engineering project to raise and reinforce embankments along Cauvery distributaries.

risk.badOutcome: High project cost keeps construction works slow in downstream blocks.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.
